summary: Dependency-confusion probe beacons internal host on npm install
details: ect-839201@100.0.3 is a reconnaissance package with no functional code — the tarball ships only package.json (declared main `index.js` is absent), description is 'Probe', author is empty, and the version is inflated to 100.0.3 in the dependency-confusion style. Its sole effect on install is a preinstall script that fires three fallback HTTP GETs (wget, then curl, then python3 urllib) to http://10.107.121.85:8000/callback_{wget,curl,py}_839201. The path encodes the package identifier, allowing the operator to confirm which internal name resolved to their public package and from which network. Installing this package leaks internal-network reachability, the installer's egress IP, and confirmation that the targeted internal name `ect-839201` was successfully shadowed — enabling targeted follow-up against that environment.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
